subject: FAR
title: Property, Plant, and Equipment — Acquisition Cost and Depreciation Methods
scenario:
Meridian Fabrication Inc. is a calendar-year manufacturer. During Year 1 the company completed two separate transactions to expand its production capacity:

1. On **April 1, Year 1**, Meridian acquired land, a building, and production equipment from Delta Holdings LLC in a single lump-sum cash purchase. Details of the acquisition appear in **Exhibit 1**.
2. On **January 2, Year 1**, Meridian purchased a new stamping machine from an unrelated vendor and placed it in service the same week. The expenditures related to the machine and its depreciation data appear in **Exhibit 2**.

Estimated useful lives and other company information appear in **Exhibit 3**.

Using the information in the exhibits, complete each work tab below. Meridian's fiscal year ends December 31.

exhibits.title: Exhibit 1: Lump-Sum Acquisition from Delta Holdings
exhibits.content:
On April 1, Year 1, Meridian paid **$1,800,000 in cash** to Delta Holdings LLC for land, a building, and production equipment acquired together in a single transaction. The transaction is an asset acquisition (not a business combination).

Independent appraisals obtained at the acquisition date:

| Asset | Appraised fair value | Seller's carrying amount |
|---|---|---|
| Land | $500,000 | $280,000 |
| Building | $1,200,000 | $850,000 |
| Production equipment | $300,000 | $190,000 |
| **Total** | **$2,000,000** | **$1,320,000** |

exhibits.title: Exhibit 2: Stamping Machine — Expenditures and Depreciation Data
exhibits.content:
Meridian purchased a stamping machine on January 2, Year 1. The invoice terms were 2/10, n/30. Meridian paid the invoice on January 8, Year 1, and took the cash discount.

**Expenditures related to the machine**

| Expenditure | Amount |
|---|---|
| Invoice price (before discount; terms 2/10, n/30) | $150,000 |
| Freight-in (shipping to Meridian's plant) | $6,000 |
| Insurance covering the machine while in transit | $1,500 |
| Installation, wiring, and pre-production test runs | $9,000 |
| Repair of damage caused by careless unloading at the loading dock | $2,700 |
| One-year insurance policy covering machine operations during Year 1 | $3,600 |
| Training of machine operators | $4,800 |

**Depreciation data — stamping machine**

| Item | Amount |
|---|---|
| Estimated useful life | 5 years |
| Estimated salvage value | $13,500 |
| Estimated total lifetime output | 100,000 units |
| Actual output — Year 1 | 22,000 units |
| Actual output — Year 2 | 26,000 units |

exhibits.title: Exhibit 3: Useful Lives and Other Company Information
exhibits.content:
| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| Building (lump-sum purchase) — estimated useful life | 25 years from the acquisition date |
| Building — estimated salvage value | $80,000 |
| Building — depreciation method | Straight-line, computed to the nearest whole month |
| Production equipment (lump-sum purchase) — depreciation | 8-year life; $30,000 salvage value; straight-line |
| Meridian's incremental borrowing rate | 7% per annum |
| Building maintenance contract (coverage begins January Year 2) | $12,000 per year |

tasks.title: Expenditures Capitalized to the Machine
tasks.instruction: Refer to **Exhibit 2**. In addition to the net invoice price, which of the following expenditures should be capitalized as part of the cost of the stamping machine? (Select all that apply.)
options: Insurance covering the machine while in transit
Installation, wiring, and pre-production test runs
Repair of damage caused by careless unloading at the loading dock
One-year insurance policy covering machine operations during Year 1
correctAnswer: Insurance covering the machine while in transit
Installation, wiring, and pre-production test runs
tasks.explanation: The cost of property, plant, and equipment includes all expenditures necessary to bring the asset to the condition and location required for its intended use. Freight-in ($6,000), insurance while in transit ($1,500), and installation, wiring, and test runs ($9,000) all meet that criterion and are capitalized. The $2,700 repair of damage caused by careless unloading was avoidable and does not add to the asset's utility, so it is expensed as incurred. The $3,600 one-year operating insurance policy and the $4,800 operator training relate to periods after the machine is ready for use and are period operating costs.
tasks.explanationJa:
有形固定資産の取得原価には、資産を**意図した用途に使用できる状態・場所に至らせるために必要な支出**をすべて含めます。

**資本化する項目**
1. 運送費（Freight-in）$6,000 — 工場までの搬入に必要
2. 輸送中の保険料 $1,500 — 搬入過程で必要
3. 据付・配線・試運転費 $9,000 — 使用可能な状態にするために必要

**費用処理する項目**
- 荷降ろし時の不注意による損傷の修理費 $2,700 — 回避可能な支出であり資産の効用を高めない（損失/費用）
- 1年分の操業保険料 $3,600 — 使用開始後の期間費用
- オペレーター研修費 $4,800 — 期間費用（資産ではなく従業員に帰属）

tasks.title: Total Capitalized Cost of the Machine
tasks.instruction: Refer to **Exhibit 2**. Compute the total amount that should be capitalized as the cost of the stamping machine (in whole dollars, no commas). The cash discount was taken; record the machine net of the discount.
tasks.explanation: Net invoice price = $150,000 × (1 − 0.02) = $147,000. Capitalized cost = $147,000 + freight-in $6,000 + insurance in transit $1,500 + installation and testing $9,000 = $163,500. The repair of unloading damage ($2,700), the one-year operating insurance ($3,600), and operator training ($4,800) are expensed as incurred.
tasks.explanationJa:
**ステップ1: 割引後の請求価格**
$150,000 × (1 − 2%) = **$147,000**（現金割引を取得したため、割引後の純額で計上）

**ステップ2: 資本化する付随費用を加算**

| 項目 | 金額 |
|---|---|
| 割引後請求価格 | $147,000 |
| 運送費 | $6,000 |
| 輸送中保険料 | $1,500 |
| 据付・試運転費 | $9,000 |
| **取得原価合計** | **$163,500** |

修理費 $2,700・操業保険料 $3,600・研修費 $4,800 は費用処理（合計 $11,100）。

tasks.title: Allocation of the Lump-Sum Purchase Price
tasks.instruction: Refer to **Exhibit 1**. Allocate the $1,800,000 lump-sum purchase price to each asset based on relative appraised fair values. Enter each allocated cost in whole dollars (no commas).
tasks.explanation: In an asset acquisition, the cost of a group of assets is allocated to the individual assets based on their relative fair values. Total appraised fair value = $500,000 + $1,200,000 + $300,000 = $2,000,000. Land: $1,800,000 × ($500,000 ÷ $2,000,000) = $450,000. Building: $1,800,000 × ($1,200,000 ÷ $2,000,000) = $1,080,000. Production equipment: $1,800,000 × ($300,000 ÷ $2,000,000) = $270,000. The seller's carrying amounts are not relevant to the buyer's measurement.

tasks.title: Depreciation Schedule — Stamping Machine
tasks.instruction: Refer to **Exhibit 2** and the capitalized cost of the stamping machine from Work Tab 2. Complete the depreciation expense schedule for Year 1 and Year 2 under each method, in whole dollars. The machine was placed in service on January 2, Year 1; treat Year 1 as a full year of depreciation.
tasks.explanation: Capitalized cost = $163,500; salvage value = $13,500; depreciable base = $150,000. Straight-line: $150,000 ÷ 5 years = $30,000 for both Year 1 and Year 2. Double-declining balance: rate = 2 ÷ 5 = 40%, applied to book value ignoring salvage. Year 1 = $163,500 × 40% = $65,400; Year 2 = ($163,500 − $65,400) × 40% = $98,100 × 40% = $39,240. Book value at the end of Year 2 ($58,860) remains above salvage value, so no limitation applies. Units of production: rate = $150,000 ÷ 100,000 units = $1.50 per unit. Year 1 = 22,000 × $1.50 = $33,000; Year 2 = 26,000 × $1.50 = $39,000.

tasks.title: Year 1 Depreciation — Building
tasks.instruction: Refer to **Exhibit 1** and **Exhibit 3**. Compute the depreciation expense on the building for Year 1, in whole dollars (no commas). The building was acquired and placed in service on April 1, Year 1, and depreciation is computed to the nearest whole month.
tasks.explanation: Allocated cost of the building (Work Tab 3) = $1,080,000. Depreciable base = $1,080,000 − $80,000 salvage = $1,000,000. Annual straight-line depreciation = $1,000,000 ÷ 25 years = $40,000. The building was in service for 9 months of Year 1 (April 1 through December 31), so Year 1 depreciation = $40,000 × 9/12 = $30,000.

tasks.title: Total Depreciation over the Asset's Life
tasks.instruction: Assume the stamping machine is held for its entire 5-year useful life and the estimates of useful life, salvage value, and total output are not revised. Which of the following statements about total accumulated depreciation over the machine's life is correct?
options: Straight-line results in the greatest total accumulated depreciation over the asset's life
Double-declining balance results in the greatest total accumulated depreciation over the asset's life
Units of production results in the greatest total accumulated depreciation over the asset's life
All three methods result in the same total accumulated depreciation over the asset's life
tasks.correctAnswer: All three methods result in the same total accumulated depreciation over the asset's life
tasks.explanation: Every systematic and rational depreciation method allocates the same total amount — the depreciable base of cost $163,500 minus salvage value $13,500, or $150,000 — over the asset's useful life. The methods differ only in the timing pattern of the expense: double-declining balance is accelerated, units of production follows usage, and straight-line is level. Total accumulated depreciation at the end of the asset's life is $150,000 under all three methods.
tasks.explanationJa:
どの減価償却方法も、耐用年数全体では同じ償却基礎額（取得原価 $163,500 − 残存価額 $13,500 = **$150,000**）を配分します。

- 定額法: 毎期均等に配分
- 二倍定率法: 初期に加速的に配分（ただし帳簿価額は残存価額を下回らない）
- 生産高比例法: 使用量に応じて配分

異なるのは**費用認識のタイミング（パターン）だけ**であり、耐用年数終了時の減価償却累計額はいずれも $150,000 で同額になります。

subject: FAR
title: Impairment of Long-Lived Assets (ASC 360) and Amortization of Intangibles
scenario:
Sterling Industrial Corp. is a calendar-year manufacturer. During the fourth quarter of Year 3, demand for the products manufactured at Sterling's Casper plant declined sharply after the plant's largest customer switched to a competing technology. Management concluded that this significant adverse change in the business climate is an indicator of impairment and tested the Casper plant asset group — which is held and used — for recoverability at December 31, Year 3.

The Casper asset group is the lowest level for which identifiable cash flows are largely independent of the cash flows of other assets. The fair values of the individual assets within the group are not determinable without undue cost and effort.

Carrying amounts of the asset group appear in **Exhibit 1**, cash flow projections and fair value data appear in **Exhibit 2**, and information about Sterling's intangible assets — which relate to a separate product line with no impairment indicators — appears in **Exhibit 3**.

Using the information in the exhibits, complete each work tab below.

exhibits.title: Exhibit 1: Casper Plant Asset Group — Carrying Amounts at December 31, Year 3
exhibits.content:
Carrying amounts **before** any impairment adjustment:

| Asset | Cost | Accumulated depreciation | Net carrying amount |
|---|---|---|---|
| Equipment | $1,600,000 | $600,000 | $1,000,000 |
| Building | $900,000 | $300,000 | $600,000 |
| Land | $400,000 | — | $400,000 |
| **Total** | **$2,900,000** | **$900,000** | **$2,000,000** |

Sterling's Distribution division asset group (net carrying amount $950,000) showed no indicators of impairment during Year 3 and was not tested.

exhibits.title: Exhibit 2: Casper Asset Group — Cash Flow Projections and Fair Value Data
exhibits.content:
Management's estimates for the Casper asset group at December 31, Year 3:

| Item | Amount |
|---|---|
| Sum of undiscounted future cash flows expected from continued use and eventual disposition | $1,850,000 |
| Present value of those cash flows, discounted at 9% (entity-specific assumptions) | $1,580,000 |
| Fair value of the asset group per independent appraisal (market participant assumptions) | $1,600,000 |
| Estimated costs to sell if the group were disposed of | $75,000 |

Management intends to continue operating the Casper plant; the asset group is held and used, and there is no plan to sell.

exhibits.title: Exhibit 3: Intangible Assets (Separate Product Line — No Impairment Indicators)
exhibits.content:
| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| Patent — acquisition | Purchased January 1, Year 1 |
| Patent — purchase price | $400,000 |
| Patent — remaining legal life at purchase date | 10 years |
| Patent — expected period of economic benefit at purchase date | 8 years |
| Patent — expected residual value | $0 |
| Trademark — carrying amount | $120,000 |
| Trademark — renewal terms | Renewable every 10 years at minimal cost; Sterling expects to renew indefinitely |

Sterling amortizes finite-lived intangible assets on a straight-line basis.

tasks.title: Recoverability Test
tasks.instruction: Refer to **Exhibit 1** and **Exhibit 2**. Based on the ASC 360 recoverability test for a long-lived asset group that is held and used, which of the following statements is correct at December 31, Year 3?
options: The carrying amount is recoverable because the undiscounted future cash flows exceed the carrying amount; no impairment loss is recognized
The carrying amount is not recoverable because it exceeds the sum of the undiscounted future cash flows; an impairment loss must be measured
The carrying amount is not recoverable because it exceeds the present value of the future cash flows; an impairment loss must be measured
tasks.correctAnswer: The carrying amount is not recoverable because it exceeds the sum of the undiscounted future cash flows; an impairment loss must be measured
tasks.explanation: For a held-and-used asset group, the recoverability test compares the carrying amount ($2,000,000) with the sum of the undiscounted future cash flows expected from use and eventual disposition ($1,850,000). Because $2,000,000 exceeds $1,850,000, the carrying amount is not recoverable, and an impairment loss must be measured. Discounted (present value) cash flows are not used in the recoverability step; they matter only in measuring fair value.
tasks.explanationJa:
保有・使用目的（held and used）の長期性資産グループの減損は**2ステップ**で判定します。

**ステップ1: 回収可能性テスト（認識判定）**
帳簿価額と**割引前**将来キャッシュ・フロー総額を比較します。

- 帳簿価額 $2,000,000 > 割引前CF $1,850,000 → **回収不能**

したがって減損損失の測定に進みます。このステップで割引後CF（現在価値）を使わない点が最重要ポイントです。割引後の数値は公正価値（測定ステップ）でのみ意味を持ちます。

tasks.title: Measurement of the Impairment Loss
tasks.instruction: Refer to **Exhibit 1** and **Exhibit 2**. Compute the impairment loss that Sterling should recognize for the Casper asset group at December 31, Year 3 (in whole dollars, no commas).
tasks.explanation: The impairment loss is the amount by which the carrying amount of the asset group exceeds its fair value: $2,000,000 − $1,600,000 = $400,000. Fair value based on market participant assumptions is the measurement attribute for a held-and-used asset group. The entity-specific present value of $1,580,000 is not fair value, and costs to sell are relevant only to assets classified as held for sale.
tasks.explanationJa:
**ステップ2: 減損損失の測定**

減損損失 = 帳簿価額 − 公正価値
= $2,000,000 − $1,600,000 = **$400,000**

測定に使うのは**市場参加者の前提に基づく公正価値**（独立鑑定 $1,600,000）です。

- 企業固有の前提による現在価値 $1,580,000 は公正価値の定義を満たさない
- 売却費用 $75,000 を控除するのは**売却目的保有（held for sale）**に分類された場合のみ。本問は保有・使用目的のため控除しない

tasks.title: Allocation of the Impairment Loss
tasks.instruction: Allocate the impairment loss from Work Tab 2 to the assets of the Casper group on a pro rata basis using their relative net carrying amounts, and compute each asset's carrying amount after the impairment. Enter all amounts in whole dollars (no commas).
tasks.explanation: The $400,000 impairment loss is allocated to the long-lived assets of the group pro rata based on their relative net carrying amounts. Equipment: $400,000 × ($1,000,000 ÷ $2,000,000) = $200,000, leaving $1,000,000 − $200,000 = $800,000. Building: $400,000 × ($600,000 ÷ $2,000,000) = $120,000, leaving $600,000 − $120,000 = $480,000. Land: $400,000 × ($400,000 ÷ $2,000,000) = $80,000, leaving $400,000 − $80,000 = $320,000. The adjusted carrying amounts total $1,600,000, which equals the asset group's fair value. Because the individual fair values are not determinable without undue cost and effort, no individual-asset floor applies.

tasks.title: Journal Entry to Record the Impairment
tasks.instruction: Prepare the journal entry to record the impairment loss at December 31, Year 3. Credit each asset account directly for its allocated share of the loss. Enter all amounts in whole dollars (no commas). Enter 0 in cells that should have no amount.
tasks.explanation: The entry debits impairment loss for $400,000 and credits each asset for its allocated share of the loss: equipment $200,000, building $120,000, and land $80,000. The impairment loss is reported in income from continuing operations. Total debits of $400,000 equal total credits of $400,000.
tasks.explanationJa:
減損計上の仕訳:

| 勘定科目 | 借方 | 貸方 |
|---|---|---|
| 減損損失（Impairment loss） | $400,000 | |
| 設備（Equipment） | | $200,000 |
| 建物（Building） | | $120,000 |
| 土地（Land） | | $80,000 |

- 減損損失は**継続事業からの利益**の区分に表示します
- 各資産の貸方金額は Work Tab 3 の按分額と一致します
- 借方合計 $400,000 = 貸方合計 $400,000

tasks.title: Year 4 Depreciation After the Impairment
tasks.instruction: After the impairment, the equipment's remaining useful life is 5 years with no salvage value, and Sterling uses the straight-line method. Compute the depreciation expense on the Casper plant equipment for Year 4 (in whole dollars, no commas).
tasks.explanation: After an impairment loss is recognized, the adjusted carrying amount becomes the asset's new cost basis, and restoration of the previously recognized loss in future periods is prohibited. Year 4 depreciation = new basis $800,000 ÷ 5 remaining years = $160,000.
tasks.explanationJa:
減損計上後は、**減損後の帳簿価額が新たな取得原価（new cost basis）**となり、以後この基礎額をもとに減価償却します。過年度に認識した減損損失の**戻入れは禁止**されています。

Year 4 減価償却費 = 新基礎額 $800,000 ÷ 残存耐用年数 5年 = **$160,000**

tasks.title: Patent Amortization for Year 3
tasks.instruction: Refer to **Exhibit 3**. Compute the amortization expense on the patent for Year 3 (in whole dollars, no commas).
tasks.explanation: A finite-lived intangible asset is amortized over its useful life to the entity, which is the shorter of its remaining legal life (10 years) and its expected period of economic benefit (8 years). Annual amortization = $400,000 ÷ 8 years = $50,000, so Year 3 amortization expense is $50,000. The trademark is renewable indefinitely at minimal cost, so its useful life is indefinite and it is not amortized.
tasks.explanationJa:
耐用年数が確定できる無形資産は、**法的耐用年数と経済的耐用年数の短い方**で償却します。

- 残存法的耐用年数: 10年
- 経済的便益の期待期間: 8年 → **短い8年を採用**

年間償却費 = $400,000 ÷ 8年 = **$50,000**（Year 3 も同額）

なお、商標権はわずかなコストで無期限に更新が見込まれるため耐用年数が「非確定（indefinite）」となり、**償却しません**（毎期の減損テスト対象）。

tasks.title: Research — Recognition of an Impairment Loss
tasks.instruction: Sterling's controller asks you to locate the authoritative guidance stating that an impairment loss shall be recognized only if the carrying amount of a long-lived asset (asset group) is not recoverable and exceeds its fair value. Cite the ASC reference in XXX-XX-XX-XX format.
tasks.explanation: ASC 360-10-35-17 states that an impairment loss shall be recognized only if the carrying amount of a long-lived asset (asset group) is not recoverable and exceeds its fair value, that recoverability is assessed using the sum of the undiscounted cash flows expected from use and eventual disposition, and that the loss is measured as the excess of the carrying amount over fair value.
tasks.explanationJa:
**ASC 360-10-35-17** が、保有・使用目的の長期性資産（資産グループ）の減損の認識・測定を規定する中心的なパラグラフです。

- 認識: 帳簿価額が回収不能（割引前将来CF合計 < 帳簿価額）で、かつ公正価値を超過する場合のみ減損損失を認識
- 測定: 帳簿価額が公正価値を超過する金額
